perturbation.original_text: 4 The Recipient shall, at any time and if so requested by the Donor, return to the Donor (or if the Donor so requests, destroy or erase) all Samples and any documents provided by the Donor recording the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 4 The Recipient shall, at any time and if so requested by the Donor, return to the Donor (or if the Donor so requests, destroy or erase) all Samples and any documents provided by the Donor recording the Information. However, the Recipient may retain copies of such materials for internal record-keeping, irrespective of the Donor's request, notwithstanding the destruction request.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by stating that the Recipient must return or destroy all samples and documents upon the Donor's request, but then allows the Recipient to retain copies regardless of the Donor's request. This undermines the Donor's right to complete control over the information and introduces uncertainty about the extent of the Recipient's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4 The Recipient shall, at any time and if so requested by the Donor, return to the Donor (or if the Donor so requests, destroy or erase) all Samples and any documents provided by the Donor recording the Information. However, the Recipient may retain copies of such materials for internal record-keeping, irrespective of the Donor's request, notwithstanding the destruction request.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a clause that allows the Recipient to retain copies of Samples and documents 'irrespective of the Donor's request'. This directly contradicts the original clause (and the contradicted text), which states the Recipient 'shall' return or destroy all samples and documents 'if so requested by the Donor'. This creates a clear conflict and uncertainty about the Recipient's obligations regarding the Donor's information, thus a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1 In consideration of the disclosure to it by the Donor of information (whether or not contained in documents) relating to a _______________________ (‘the Information’) for the purposes of the preparation, filing and prosecution of one or more patent applications (‘the Applications’) on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information and it will not without the ex press prior consent of the Donor either:
perturbation.changed_text: 1 In consideration of the disclosure to it by the Donor of information (whether or not contained in documents) relating to a _______________________ (‘the Information’) for the purposes of the preparation, filing and prosecution of one or more patent applications (‘the Applications’) on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information, however, the recipient reserves the right to disclose such information without prior consent if compelled by a subpoena or by lawful requests of government authorities, irrespective of the conditions outlined in the following subsections.
perturbation.explanation: This introduces an in-text contradiction. The first part states the Recipient undertakes to preserve confidentiality and not disclose without the Donor's consent. The added clause then states the Recipient can disclose without consent if compelled by subpoena or government authorities, directly contradicting the initial undertaking.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1 In consideration of the disclosure to it by the Donor of information (whether or not contained in documents) relating to a _______________________ (‘the Information’) for the purposes of the preparation, filing and prosecution of one or more patent applications (‘the Applications’) on behalf of the Donor (‘the Purpose’) the Recipient undertakes that it will respect and preserve the confidentiality of the Information, however, the recipient reserves the right to disclose such information without prior consent if compelled by a subpoena or by lawful requests of government authorities, irrespective of the conditions outlined in the following subsections.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an in-text contradiction by stating that the Recipient can disclose confidential information without the Donor's consent if compelled by a subpoena or government authorities. This directly contradicts the initial undertaking that the Recipient will respect and preserve confidentiality and will not disclose without the Donor's consent. The changed text and contradicted text are in the same location and are directly contradicting each other. As per the explanation given, this is indeed an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3 The above obligations shall also apply to any sample or article incorporating or derived from the Information and whether or not provided by the Donor (‘Samples’).
perturbation.changed_text: 3 The above obligations shall also apply to any sample or article incorporating or derived from the Information and whether or not provided by the Donor (‘Samples’). However, the Recipient is not liable for safeguarding samples that are lost, stolen or damaged due to circumstances beyond their direct control, contradicting previously mentioned obligations.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by adding a clause that excuses the Recipient from safeguarding Samples under certain circumstances (lost, stolen, or damaged due to uncontrollable events). This directly contradicts the previously established obligations to safeguard samples, creating ambiguity about the extent of the Recipient's responsibility. This diminishes the Donor's protection.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3 The above obligations shall also apply to any sample or article incorporating or derived from the Information and whether or not provided by the Donor (‘Samples’). However, the Recipient is not liable for safeguarding samples that are lost, stolen or damaged due to circumstances beyond their direct control, contradicting previously mentioned obligations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat that the Recipient is not liable for safeguarding samples under certain circumstances (loss, theft, or damage due to uncontrollable events). The contradicted text states that 'The above obligations shall also apply to any sample or article...'. The added caveat creates a direct contradiction, undermining the previously established obligation to safeguard samples and creating ambiguity about the extent of the Recipient's responsibility. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections of a contract provide conflicting obligations.
